name: Charlie Kolar
headline: Efficient blocking tight end with too little volume to matter
body:
The advanced profile screams efficiency without opportunity. Across 13 games in 2025, Kolar ran routes on just 24.4% of dropbacks, drew 15 targets (a 3.7% share), and turned 10 catches into 142 yards and two scores. He was excellent on contact, posting a 66.7% success rate and 0.344 EPA per play, and the prior two seasons were even sharper (0.744 and 0.854 EPA). But a 0.133 target-per-route rate and 0.077 WOPR confirm he is a part-time receiver, not a featured one.

The move to the Chargers does nothing to unlock that ceiling. He profiles as a blocking-first body buried behind Oronde Gadsden, with modest projections of roughly 16 catches, 200 yards and a couple of touchdowns. ADP near pick 270 reflects the reality: he is a deep-league afterthought whose efficiency never gets enough snaps to translate into fantasy points.
